title: calceolaria adenanthera subsp. bracteata
intro: Calceolaria adenanthera subsp. bracteata, also known as the Slipper Flower, is a species of flowering plant that belongs to the Calceolaria genus in the family Plantaginaceae. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The Slipper Flower has been used to alleviate symptoms of colds and flu, as well as to soothe digestive issues, with its herbal preparations often consumed as teas or infusions. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared by drying its flowers and leaves, which are then used to make herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures to harness its medicinal benefits.
